behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests check for very specific strings like 'pad_string.insert(pad_string_.begin(), pad_string_.end())', 'chassert(column_pad_const)', 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string', 'insertFromItself', exact include lines like '<memory>' and '<DataTypes/IDataType.h>', and 'const ColumnsWithTypeAndName & arguments'. While some of these are mentioned in instruction.md, others like the exact insert call signature, chassert with specific argument, ColumnsWithTypeAndName signature change, and the exact private m

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent exactly what to do: use PaddedPODArray<UInt8> for pad_string, use insertFromItself for expansion, use chassert(column_pad_const), use validateFunctionArguments with FunctionArgumentDescriptors, use explicit 'num_chars == 0', use digit separators '1'000'000', use size() not length(), include <memory> and <DataTypes/IDataType.h>, remove specific error codes. This is essentially a step-by-step implementation guide, not a problem description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact string 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' in private section, exact string 'pad_string.insert(pad_string_.begin(), pad_string_.end())', 'insertFromItself', 'chassert(column_pad_const)', 'const ColumnsWithTypeAndName & arguments', digit separator '1'000'000'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. a different container with padding guarantee, different initialization method, or different variable naming would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test reads the source file and checks for literal strings using pattern matching (e.g., 'if "PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string" not in content'). No test compiles code, runs the functions, or checks actual runtime behavior. All tests are pure text-matching on source code.

test_not_tautological.reason: An agent could simply write the file with all the expected string patterns without understanding the bug. Since tests only check for text presence in the source file, copying the exact expected strings into the file would pass all tests without any functional correctness.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 11 requirements listed in instruction.md are tested: PaddedPODArray usage, empty pad string handling, validateFunctionArguments, chassert, explicit comparison, digit separators, size() method, includes, brace style, error code removal, and insertFromItself.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive names (test_padded_pod_array_storage, test_empty_pad_string_handling, etc.), docstrings explaining each test's purpose, and clear PASS/FAIL messages.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces modified C++ source code, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No obvious typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the exact file (src/Functions/padString.cpp), exact class (PaddingChars), exact member (pad_string), exact methods to use (insertFromItself, chassert, validateFunctionArguments), exact error codes to remove, and exact includes to add. This goes far beyond identifying the buggy area.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config/agentmd-edits task.
